incident_text: An employee opened an attachment, after which local and network files were encrypted and normal access stopped. The event was escalated because the affected system processed sensitive information.
A profile field stored active client-side markup that triggered when the page loaded. Relevant authentication, endpoint, and access logs were retained for investigation.
A malicious process encrypted shared files and deleted accessible backup copies before displaying recovery instructions.
An invoice-themed email delivered a malicious attachment that encrypted local files and displayed a payment demand. The organization preserved the available evidence and began containment.

Compromised devices received instructions from control infrastructure and launched a coordinated flood against a public service. The incident required coordinated investigation across identity, endpoint, and network teams.
The public service received an extreme surge of requests from distributed sources and became unavailable to legitimate users. The source had not previously been associated with normal activity.
The public application received crafted requests that exploited an input-validation weakness and exposed protected application data.
A deceptive email delivered malicious software that collected and transmitted sensitive documents. The incident required coordinated investigation across identity, endpoint, and network teams.

Compromised devices received instructions from control infrastructure and launched a coordinated flood against a public service. The organization preserved the available evidence and began containment.
A deceptive email delivered malicious software that collected and transmitted sensitive documents. The affected service was temporarily restricted while analysts assessed the impact.
A remote source scanned exposed services and then exploited a vulnerable web endpoint discovered during reconnaissance. Additional related activity was found during the initial review.
The account was locked after a high-rate sequence of invalid credentials from an unfamiliar address.
A downloaded document attempted to run an embedded script and install a background process. Analysts requested containment and a review of related accounts and systems.
A vulnerable page stored unsanitized browser code that executed when other users opened the affected content. The affected service was temporarily restricted while analysts assessed the impact.

Compromised devices received instructions from control infrastructure and launched a coordinated flood against a public service. The incident required coordinated investigation across identity, endpoint, and network teams.
An internal user altered protected records despite having no assigned responsibility for those accounts.
